content.problemStatement:
Chef works at a restaurant where he gets paid ₹X\char"20B9 X₹X per hour.

One day, he visits a gift shop where there are NNN gifts.

The price of the ithi^{th}ith gift is ₹Ai\char"20B9 A_i₹Ai​. Each gift can be bought **at most once**.

Currently, Chef has no money. So, he wants to find the **minimum** number of hours he needs to work at the restaurant such that he can save **exactly** ₹Z\char"20B9 Z₹Z after buying any (possibly zero) number of gifts from the shop.

Help him find this value or report that it is impossible to do so.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, XXX and ZZZ — the number of gifts, Chef's earnings per hour and the amount of savings he wants to have in the end.
The next line contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the price of the gifts.




Output Format
For each test case, if it is impossible to achieve the goal, print −1-1−1. Otherwise, print the **minimum** number of hours he needs to work.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef can work for 333 hours and earn ₹15\char"20B9 15₹15.
He can then buy the first two gifts and spend a total of ₹3\char"20B9 3₹3 on them. His total savings will thus be exact ₹12\char"20B9 12₹12.
Test case 222: Chef can work for 222 hours and earn ₹8\char"20B9 8₹8. He doesn't need to buy anything to have savings of ₹8\char"20B9 8₹8.
Test case 333: No matter how many hours Chef works or how many gifts he buys, he cannot save exactly ₹12\char"20B9 12₹12 in the end.
